purpose: Strategic frameworks and insight models for the strategic-insights skill. Used to surface high-leverage career and professional growth opportunities from conversation patterns.

hidden_strength_signals: Consistently solves a certain type of problem faster or better than expected
Is sought out informally for a specific type of advice or decision
Describes certain tasks as 'easy' or 'obvious' that others find difficult
Has domain knowledge that is rare in their current context
Receives repeated positive feedback on a specific behavior but hasn't formalized it
Makes connections across domains that others miss
underuse_signals: A strength only applied in narrow context when it could have broader impact
Expertise kept mostly internal when it could build external visibility
A skill used reactively when it could be used proactively as a strategic differentiator
High capability in an area that is currently misaligned with career goals
blind_spot_signals: Repeating the same struggle across different contexts
Describing luck or chance where strategic positioning likely played a role
Attributing others' success to traits the user also demonstrates
Investing effort in areas with low leverage while high-leverage options are visible

ikigai.description: Japanese concept: the intersection of what you love, what you're good at, what the world needs, and what you can be paid for

quadrants.love_and_good_at: Passion — fulfilling but may lack market value
quadrants.good_at_and_paid: Profession — sustainable but may lack meaning
quadrants.paid_and_needed: Vocation — impactful but may not energize you
quadrants.needed_and_love: Mission — meaningful but may not pay yet

ikigai.insight_prompt: Where is the user strongest? What quadrant are they in now? What shift would move them toward the center?
ikigai.use_when: User is navigating a career transition or feels misaligned

strength_zone.description: Adapted from Gay Hendricks' 'The Big Leap' — four zones of work

zones.zone_of_incompetence: Work you do poorly — delegate or eliminate
zones.zone_of_competence: Work you do fine but others can do equally well — minimize
zones.zone_of_excellence: Work you do very well and are rewarded for — the trap that prevents growth
zones.zone_of_genius: Work that uses unique gifts, feels effortless, creates disproportionate value — maximize

strength_zone.insight_prompt: What zone is the user spending most time in? What's one move toward Zone of Genius this week?
strength_zone.use_when: User is high-performer looking for the next level of leverage

career_capital.description: From Cal Newport's 'So Good They Can't Ignore You' — build rare and valuable skills before seeking autonomy and meaning

types.skills: Deep technical, analytical, or domain expertise
types.relationships: Network of people who can create opportunities
types.reputation: Track record and credibility in a specific domain

career_capital.insight_prompt: What type of career capital is the user building? What type is underinvested? What's the highest-ROI investment this week?
career_capital.use_when: User is early-to-mid career or seeking to change roles

visibility_vs_impact.description: Many professionals have high impact but low visibility — or invest in visibility without impact. The goal is both.

quadrants.high_impact_low_visibility: Doing great work no one knows about — strategic communication needed
quadrants.high_visibility_low_impact: Getting attention without results — focus needed

visibility_vs_impact.insight_prompt: Where does the user sit in this matrix? What one action shifts them toward high-impact high-visibility?
visibility_vs_impact.use_when: User mentions not being recognized, being overlooked, or struggling to influence

contribution_ladder.description: Five levels of professional contribution — most people plateau at their current level and don't see the next rung

levels.2: Anticipate what's needed without being asked
levels.3: Identify and solve problems before they escalate
levels.4: Create systems that multiply others' work
levels.5: Shape strategy and build organizational capability

contribution_ladder.insight_prompt: What level is the user operating at most of the time? What would level-up look like? What's one concrete behavior that demonstrates the next level?
contribution_ladder.use_when: User wants to get promoted or increase their scope of influence

principles: One behavior only — not a new mindset, a new habit, or a new goal
7 days exactly — short enough to commit, long enough to generate signal
Measurable — must be binary (did/did not) or a simple count
Low friction — under 15 minutes per day
Linked to the identified insight — the experiment tests the insight directly

communication: Send a 3-bullet weekly summary to manager every Friday
Speak first in the next 3 team meetings
Ask one clarifying question before taking on any new task
visibility: Share one insight or result per week in team channel
Write a 2-sentence summary of completed work at end of each day
Request to present one topic at next team sync
focus: Identify 3 priorities each morning before opening email
Block 60-minute deep work window at fixed time each day
Use a daily NOT-to-do list for reactive low-value tasks
relationships: One outreach per day to someone you've lost touch with (3 sentences max)
Ask one question in every meeting that shows you've read the pre-read
Send a specific thank-you or recognition to a peer once per day
decision_quality: Write the decision and one-sentence rationale before acting
Set a 24-hour pause on any reversible decision you feel pressure to make instantly
Track every decision made today — review at end of week for patterns

output_quality_rules.specificity: Insight must reference specific observed behavior, not generic personality description. Bad: 'You are strategic.' Good: 'You consistently frame problems at a higher level than the question asked — that's a rare thinking pattern.'
output_quality_rules.brevity: Insight: max 3 sentences. Weekly change: one sentence. Tracking: 2 lines.
output_quality_rules.actionability: Experiment must be something the user can start tomorrow with no preparation.
output_quality_rules.non_judgmental_tone: Describe patterns without judgment. 'You tend to...' not 'You fail to...' or 'You are bad at...'
